session_context: June 19 session overlay: risk-on emerging; gap narrowing; medium confidence, emerging stability.

drafts.x:
Oil is cratering — and the other signals aren't confirming.
Only matters if yields confirms.

SPX 7,511. 10Y 4.49%. Oil $76.

Invalidation:
• 10Y above 4.64% + SPX loses 7,436
• Oil above $85

What's the signal that tells you this rally is real?
drafts.substack:
REGIME: Risk-on | Confirmed
CONFIDENCE: Medium (43/100)
RISK TILT: pro-risk
STABILITY: Emerging

Snapshot — Jun 19, 2026
  S&P 500:     7,511.35   z +1.01
  10Y Yield:      4.487%  z +0.75
  WTI Oil:        76.05   z -2.72

INSIGHT
S&P 500 at 7,511 is elevated relative to its rolling mean — starting to lean but not decisive. 10Y yield at 4.49% is sitting near its 63-session mean — no signal from yields. Oil at $76 is running well below its 63-session range — oil haven't been this stretched in months. Confidence sits at 43/100 — enough for a directional lean but not a high-conviction call.

INVALIDATION
• 10Y above 4.64% + SPX loses 7,436
• Oil above $85

Method: MacroEvidence pipeline · yfinance + FRED · 63-session rolling z-scores · not financial advice.

inv_rows.condition: SPX above 7,582 or below 7,436 → Regime flips

regime_analogs.intro: As of June 19, 2026, three closest structural matches for today’s risk-on emerging regime — gap narrowing at 0.26σ, WTI at Z -2.72 non-confirming. Members search the full 50-year database.

analog_rows.outcome: Regime confirmed pro-risk — SPX cleared the confirmation gate within 4 sessions as 10Y held steady and oil stayed suppressed without driving the read
Regime invalidated — 10Y broke above the upper threshold as SPX failed the gate, oil’s extreme low did not prevent a defensive reset
Regime held Emerging for 6 sessions before resolving pro-risk — gap closed gradually as SPX ground higher and 10Y stayed range-bound

regime_analogs.context_note: Structural pattern matches only — not predictions. Gap at 0.26σ with WTI at Z -2.72 (non-confirming outlier) is the defining condition. Resolution at 7,582 and 4.64%. Historical context.
